identity.short_name: Involutions in truncated-ring automorphisms -> Gaussian-binomial codim-2 subspace count

subdomain(s): composition of truncated power series over finite fields
finite abelian groups and invariant factors
quotienting by a fixed cyclic subgroup

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count order-2 automorphisms of F7[x]/(x^6)); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Count HG with gH and G/H(2^5,2^2), mod 1000) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Count involutions in Aut(F7[x]/(x^3)) by composing truncated series to obtain T, then count codimension-2 subspaces containing a fixed vector in F2^T using Gaussian binomial coefficients; output mod 1000.
givens: Involution means phi_{a,b}^2=id; T counts such automorphisms.
V=F_2^T with fixed g0; N counts subspaces H containing g with dim(V/H)=2.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: finite-field base for the truncated ring

parameter(s).name: series_coefficients
parameter(s).purpose: parametrizes automorphisms via the image of x

parameter(s).name: G_invariant_exponents
parameter(s).domain: nonincreasing tuple (a1,...,ar) with ai>=1 defining G Z/2^{ai}Z

parameter(s).name: fixed_element_description
parameter(s).domain: specified element g in G, typically a 2^t multiple of a generator in one summand

parameter(s).name: Q_invariant_exponents
parameter(s).domain: nonincreasing tuple (b1,...,bs) with bj>=1 defining Q Z/2^{bj}Z

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: automorphism_parameterization
invariant(s).statement: is an F_p-algebra automorphism iff (x)=a x + higher terms (mod x^n) with aF_p^.

invariant(s).name: composition_mod_truncation
invariant(s).statement: ^k=id is equivalent to f^{k}(x)  x (mod x^n) where f(x)=(x) as a truncated series.

invariant(s).name: leading_coefficient_constraint
invariant(s).statement: From the x-coefficient in f^{k}(x)x, one gets a^k=1 in F_p^ (here a^2=1).

invariant(s).name: triangular_coefficient_system
invariant(s).statement: Expanding f^{2}(x) mod x^n yields a (nearly) triangular system of polynomial equations in b,c,d,e solvable by casework on a.

invariant(s).name: containment_reduction_to_quotient
invariant(s).statement: Subgroups HG with gH and G/HQ correspond to kernels of surjections f:GQ with f(g)=0, equivalently to surjections G/gQ. Hence N = |Epi(G/g,Q)| / |Aut(Q)|.

invariant(s).name: hom_count_depends_only_on_invariant_factors
invariant(s).statement: |Hom(G,Q)| is determined by the exponents via |Hom(Z/2^a,Q)| = |Q[2^a]| = 2^{Σ_j min(a,bj)}.

invariant(s).name: epimorphisms_via_maximal_subgroups_rank2
invariant(s).statement: For rank-2 Q, maximal subgroups have index 2 and correspond to the 3 nonzero lines in Q/2Q(Z/2)^2; inclusionexclusion over these maximal subgroups yields |Epi(G,Q)|.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Vector spaces are over F2; g is nonzero.
Automorphisms are F_7-algebra automorphisms (fix scalars in F_7).
Composition is computed in R, i.e., all series are truncated mod x^6.
All groups are finite abelian 2-groups written in invariant-factor form.
The condition gH is interpreted literally as subgroup containment.
Aut(Q) acts freely on Epi(G,Q) by postcomposition, so dividing counts kernels.
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The involution constraints determine a finite count T uniquely.
Given T, the Gaussian binomial coefficient gives a unique N.

Each automorphism corresponds to a unique coefficient tuple (a,b,c,d,e); the system f^{2}(x)x (mod x^6) has finitely many solutions in F_7, so |I| is uniquely determined once the coefficient system is solved.

The constraint gH forces reduction to G' = G/g. The kernel of any surjection G'Q gives such an H, and two surjections have the same kernel iff they differ by Aut(Q). Since |Epi(G',Q)| and |Aut(Q)| are uniquely determined from invariant factors and maximal-subgroup inclusionexclusion, N mod 1000 is unique.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: order_condition_in_truncated_automorphism_group
technique(s).purpose: translate phi^2=id into algebraic equations over F7

technique(s).name: surjection_over_aut_gaussian_count
technique(s).purpose: count subspaces via quotienting by <g> and Gaussian coefficients

technique(s).name: translate_order_condition_to_series_identity
outputs: coefficient equations in F_7 for a,b,c,d,e

technique(s).name: solve_linear_term_first
technique(s).purpose: restrict a to square roots of unity in F_7^

technique(s).name: casework_by_a
technique(s).purpose: simplify the remaining coefficient equations (some factors vanish depending on a)

technique(s).name: count_free_parameters
technique(s).purpose: count solutions as powers of 7 when variables remain free
outputs: solution count per case and total

technique(s).name: reduce_by_fixed_element
technique(s).purpose: replace the constrained subgroup count by an unconstrained count on G' = G/g

technique(s).name: kernel_surjection_correspondence
technique(s).purpose: convert subgroup counting to counting surjections modulo Aut(Q)

technique(s).name: count_hom_sets_by_invariant_factors
technique(s).purpose: compute |Hom(Gprime,Q)| and similar Hom-counts to maximal subgroups

technique(s).name: epimorphism_count_via_maximal_subgroups
technique(s).purpose: compute |Epi(Gprime,Q)| using inclusionexclusion over index-2 maximal subgroups of Q

technique(s).name: compute_aut_size_for_rank2_2group
technique(s).purpose: compute |Aut(Q)| by counting endomorphisms with invertible action on Q/2Q

theorem(s).name: finite_field_equation_solving
theorem(s).role: solve a^2=1 and linear constraints over F7

theorem(s).name: Gaussian_binomial_formula
theorem(s).role: count subspaces in finite vector spaces

theorem(s).name: Units criterion for truncated polynomial automorphisms
theorem(s).role: f(x) defines an automorphism iff its linear term is a unit in F_p

theorem(s).name: Finite-field root counting for a^2=1
theorem(s).role: determine the admissible a values in F_7^

theorem(s).name: first_isomorphism_theorem
theorem(s).role: kernels of surjections correspond to quotient isomorphism types

theorem(s).name: classification_of_finite_abelian_p_groups
theorem(s).role: ensures invariant-factor form and Hom-count formulas

theorem(s).name: maximal_subgroups_via_Q_mod_2Q
theorem(s).role: maximal subgroups of a rank-2 2-group correspond to lines in Q/2Q

theorem(s).name: endomorphism_is_automorphism_iff_mod_2_is_invertible
theorem(s).role: for finite 2-groups, surjectivity is equivalent to surjectivity on Q/2Q (Burnside/Frattini viewpoint)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Function composition as an algebraic constraint (coefficient comparison)

critical_trick(s): Compute phi^2(x) explicitly modulo x^3 to derive involution equations.
Use the involution count T as the dimension parameter in the F2-subspace problem.
Apply Gaussian binomial counting after quotienting by the fixed line <g>.
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

library(s).name: sage
library(s).reason: model R, compose series mod x^6, and count solutions over F_7 exactly (optional but convenient)

import_hint(s): # sage: F = GF(7); PR.<x> = PolynomialRing(F); S = PR.quotient(x^6)
# sage: define f = a*x + b*x^2 + c*x^3 + d*x^4 + e*x^5 in S

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
